Takes a seed scenario and iteratively generates situations across 12 dimensions — happy paths, errors, edge cases, abuse, scale, concurrency, temporal, data variation, permissions, integrations, recovery, and state transitions. ``` /autoresearch:scenario Scenario: User attempts to checkout with multiple payment methods Iterations: 25 ``` **How it works:** Seed analysis → Decompose into 12 dimensions → Generate ONE situation per iteration → Classify (new/variant/duplicate) → Expand edge cases → Log → Repeat until all dimensions explored. Adaptive setup: provides 4-8 questions based on how much context you give. Just type `/autoresearch:scenario` with nothing else and it walks you through everything. | Flag | Purpose | |------|---------| | `--domain ` | Domain: software, product, business, security, marketing | | `--depth ` | Depth: shallow (10), standard (25), deep (50+) | | `--format ` | Output: use-cases, user-stories, test-scenarios, threat-scenarios | | `--focus ` | Prioritize: edge-cases, failures, security, scale | | `--scope ` | Limit to specific files/features | **5 domains supported** with tailored dimension priorities and output formats. **Chain with** `/autoresearch:debug` to hunt bugs in discovered edge cases, or `/autoresearch:security` to audit discovered threat scenarios. --- ## Guard — Prevent Regressions (v1.0.4) When optimizing a metric, the loop might break existing behavior. **Guard** is an optional safety net. ``` /autoresearch Goal: Reduce API response time to under 100ms Verify: npm run bench:api | grep "p95" Guard: npm test ``` - **Verify** = "Did the metric improve?" (the goal) - **Guard** = "Did anything else break?" (the safety net) If the metric improves but the guard fails, Claude reworks the optimization (up to 2 attempts). Guard/test files are never modified. > **Credit:** Guard was contributed by [@pronskiy](https://github.com/pronskiy) (JetBrains) in [PR #7](https://github.com/uditgoenka/autoresearch/pull/7). --- ## Results Tracking Every iteration is logged in TSV format: ```tsv iteration commit metric delta status description 0 a1b2c3d 85.2 0.0 baseline initial state 1 b2c3d4e 87.1 +1.9 keep add tests for auth edge cases 2 - 86.5 -0.6 discard refactor test helpers (broke 2 tests) 3 c3d4e5f 88.3 +1.2 keep add error handling tests ``` Every 10 iterations, Claude prints a progress summary. Bounded loops print a final summary with baseline → current best. --- ## Crash Recovery | Failure | Response | |---------|----------| | Syntax error | Fix immediately, don't count as iteration | | Runtime error | Attempt fix (max 3 tries), then move on | | Resource exhaustion | Revert, try smaller variant | | Infinite loop / hang | Kill after timeout, revert | | External dependency | Skip, log, try different approach | --- ## Repository Structure ``` autoresearch/ ├── README.md ├── COMPARISON.md ← Karpathy's Autoresearch vs Claude Autoresearch ├── guide/ ← Comprehensive guides — one per command + advanced patterns │ ├── README.md ← Guide index │ ├── getting-started.md ← Installation, core concepts, FAQ │ ├── autoresearch.md ← The autonomous loop │ ├── autoresearch-plan.md ← Setup wizard │ ├── autoresearch-debug.md ← Bug hunter │ ├── autoresearch-fix.md ← Error crusher │ ├── autoresearch-security.md ← Security auditor │ ├── autoresearch-ship.md ← Shipping workflow │ ├── autoresearch-scenario.md ← Scenario explorer │ ├── autoresearch-predict.md ← Multi-persona swarm prediction │ ├── autoresearch-learn.md ← Documentation engine │ ├── chains-and-combinations.md ← Multi-command pipelines │ ├── examples-by-domain.md ← Real-world examples by domain │ ├── advanced-patterns.md ← Guards, MCP, CI/CD, FAQ │ └── scenario/ ← 10 real-world scenario walkthroughs │ ├── README.md ← Scenario guide index │ ├── real-time-chat-messaging.md │ ├── multi-tenant-saas-onboarding.md │ ├── cicd-pipeline-deployment.md │ ├── healthcare-appointment-scheduling.md │ ├── social-media-content-moderation.md │ ├── iot-firmware-updates.md │ ├── document-collaboration.md │ ├── cross-border-wire-transfers.md │ ├── search-autocomplete.md │ └── mobile-push-notifications.md ├── LICENSE ├── .claude-plugin/ │ └── marketplace.json ← Plugin marketplace manifest (source: ./claude-plugin) ├── claude-plugin/ ← Distribution package (what users install) │ ├── .claude-plugin/ │ │ └── plugin.json ← Plugin metadata + version │ ├── commands/ │ │ ├── autoresearch.md ← Main /autoresearch command │ │ └── autoresearch/ │ │ ├── ship.md ← /autoresearch:ship registration │ │ ├── plan.md ← /autoresearch:plan registration │ │ ├── security.md ← /autoresearch:security registration │ │ ├── debug.md ← /autoresearch:debug registration │ │ ├── fix.md ← /autoresearch:fix registration │ │ ├── scenario.md ← /autoresearch:scenario registration │ │ ├── predict.md ← /autoresearch:predict registration │ │ └── learn.md ← /autoresearch:learn registration │ └── skills/ │ └── autoresearch/ │ ├── SKILL.md ← Main skill (loaded by Claude Code) │ └── references/ │ ├── autonomous-loop-protocol.md ← 8-phase loop protocol │ ├── core-principles.md ← 7 universal principles │ ├── plan-workflow.md ← Plan wizard protocol │ ├── security-workflow.md ← Security audit protocol │ ├── ship-workflow.md ← Ship workflow protocol │ ├── debug-workflow.md ← Debug loop protocol │ ├── fix-workflow.md ← Fix loop protocol │ ├── scenario-workflow.md ← Scenario exploration protocol │ ├── predict-workflow.md ← Multi-persona swarm prediction workflow │ ├── learn-workflow.md ← Documentation engine protocol │ └── results-logging.md ← TSV tracking format ``` --- ## FAQ **Q: I don't know what metric to use.** A: Run `/autoresearch:plan` — it analyzes your codebase, suggests metrics, and dry-runs the verify command before you launch. **Q: Does this work with any project?** A: Yes. Any language, framework, or domain. Install via `/plugin marketplace add uditgoenka/autoresearch` or manually copy from the `claude-plugin/` directory. **Q: How do I stop the loop?** A: `Ctrl+C` or add `Iterations: N` to your inline config to run exactly N iterations. Claude commits before verifying, so your last successful state is always in git. **Q: Can I use this for non-code tasks?** A: Absolutely. Sales emails, marketing copy, HR policies, runbooks — anything with a measurable metric. See [Examples by Domain](guide/examples-by-domain.md). **Q: Does /autoresearch:security modify my code?** A: No. It's read-only — analyzes code and produces a structured report. Use `--fix` to opt into auto-remediation of confirmed Critical/High findings. **Q: Can I use MCP servers?** A: Yes. Any MCP server configured in Claude Code is available during the loop for database queries, API calls, analytics, etc. See [Advanced Patterns](guide/advanced-patterns.md#using-with-mcp-servers). --- ## Contributing Contributions welcome! See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md). Areas of interest: new domain examples, verification script templates, CI/CD integrations, real-world benchmarks. All guides are in the [guide/](guide/) folder. --- ## Star History --- ## License MIT — see [LICENSE](LICENSE). --- ## Credits - **[Andrej Karpathy](https://github.com/karpathy)** — for [autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch) - **[Anthropic](https://anthropic.com)** — for [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) and the skills system ---
## About the Author **[Udit Goenka](https://udit.co)** — AI Product Expert, Founder & Angel Investor Self-taught builder who went from a slow internet connection in India to founding multiple companies and helping 700+ startups generate over ~$25m in revenue. **Building:** [TinyCheque](https://tinycheque.com) (India's first agentic AI venture studio) · [Firstsales.io](https://firstsales.io) (sales automation) **Investing:** 38 startups backed, 6 exits. Focused on early-stage AI and SaaS. **Connect:** [udit.co](https://udit.co) · [@iuditg](https://x.com/iuditg) · [@uditgoenka](https://github.com/uditgoenka) · [Newsletter](https://udit.co/blog) > *"Autonomy scales when you constrain scope, clarify success, mechanize verification, and let agents optimize tactics while humans optimize strategy."*

content.text:
Lightpanda Browser

The headless browser built from scratch for AI agents and automation.
Not a Chromium fork. Not a WebKit patch. A new browser, written in Zig.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/github/license/lightpanda-io/browser)](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/blob/main/LICENSE) [![Twitter Follow](https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/lightpanda_io)](https://twitter.com/lightpanda_io) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/lightpanda-io/browser)](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser) [![Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/1391984864894521354?style=flat-square&label=discord)](https://discord.gg/K63XeymfB5)
[ ](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo)   [ ](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo)
_chromedp requesting 933 real web pages over the network on a AWS EC2 m5.large instance. See [benchmark details](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo/blob/main/BENCHMARKS.md#crawler-benchmark)._ Lightpanda is the open-source browser made for headless usage: - Javascript execution - Support of Web APIs (partial, WIP) - Compatible with Playwright[^1], Puppeteer, chromedp through [CDP](https://chromedevtools.github.io/devtools-protocol/) Fast web automation for AI agents, LLM training, scraping and testing: - Ultra-low memory footprint (9x less than Chrome) - Exceptionally fast execution (11x faster than Chrome) - Instant startup [^1]: **Playwright support disclaimer:** Due to the nature of Playwright, a script that works with the current version of the browser may not function correctly with a future version. Playwright uses an intermediate JavaScript layer that selects an execution strategy based on the browser's available features. If Lightpanda adds a new [Web API](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API), Playwright may choose to execute different code for the same script. This new code path could attempt to use features that are not yet implemented. Lightpanda makes an effort to add compatibility tests, but we can't cover all scenarios. If you encounter an issue, please create a [GitHub issue](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/issues) and include the last known working version of the script. ## Quick start ### Install **Install from the nightly builds** You can download the last binary from the [nightly builds](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/releases/tag/nightly) for Linux x86_64 and MacOS aarch64. *For Linux* ```console curl -L -o lightpanda https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/releases/download/nightly/lightpanda-x86_64-linux && \ chmod a+x ./lightpanda ``` *For MacOS* ```console curl -L -o lightpanda https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/releases/download/nightly/lightpanda-aarch64-macos && \ chmod a+x ./lightpanda ``` *For Windows + WSL2* The Lightpanda browser is compatible to run on windows inside WSL. Follow the Linux instruction for installation from a WSL terminal. It is recommended to install clients like Puppeteer on the Windows host. **Install from Docker** Lightpanda provides [official Docker images](https://hub.docker.com/r/lightpanda/browser) for both Linux amd64 and arm64 architectures. The following command fetches the Docker image and starts a new container exposing Lightpanda's CDP server on port `9222`. ```console docker run -d --name lightpanda -p 9222:9222 lightpanda/browser:nightly ``` ### Dump a URL ```console ./lightpanda fetch --obey-robots --log-format pretty --log-level info https://demo-browser.lightpanda.io/campfire-commerce/ ``` ```console INFO telemetry : telemetry status . . . . . . . . . . . . . [+0ms] disabled = false INFO page : navigate . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [+6ms] url = https://demo-browser.lightpanda.io/campfire-commerce/ method = GET reason = address_bar body = false req_id = 1 INFO browser : executing script . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [+118ms] src = https://demo-browser.lightpanda.io/campfire-commerce/script.js kind = javascript cacheable = true INFO http : request complete . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [+140ms] source = xhr url = https://demo-browser.lightpanda.io/campfire-commerce/json/product.json status = 200 len = 4770 INFO http : request complete . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [+141ms] source = fetch url = https://demo-browser.lightpanda.io/campfire-commerce/json/reviews.json status = 200 len = 1615 ``` ### Start a CDP server ```console ./lightpanda serve --obey-robots --log-format pretty --log-level info --host 127.0.0.1 --port 9222 ``` ```console INFO telemetry : telemetry status . . . . . . . . . . . . . [+0ms] disabled = false INFO app : server running . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . [+0ms] address = 127.0.0.1:9222 ``` Once the CDP server started, you can run a Puppeteer script by configuring the `browserWSEndpoint`. ```js 'use strict' import puppeteer from 'puppeteer-core'; // use browserWSEndpoint to pass the Lightpanda's CDP server address. const browser = await puppeteer.connect({ browserWSEndpoint: "ws://127.0.0.1:9222", }); // The rest of your script remains the same. const context = await browser.createBrowserContext(); const page = await context.newPage(); // Dump all the links from the page. await page.goto('https://demo-browser.lightpanda.io/amiibo/', {waitUntil: "networkidle0"}); const links = await page.evaluate(() => { return Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('a')).map(row => { return row.getAttribute('href'); }); }); console.log(links); await page.close(); await context.close(); await browser.disconnect(); ``` ### Telemetry By default, Lightpanda collects and sends usage telemetry. This can be disabled by setting an environment variable `LIGHTPANDA_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=true`. You can read Lightpanda's privacy policy at: [https://lightpanda.io/privacy-policy](https://lightpanda.io/privacy-policy). ## Status Lightpanda is in Beta and currently a work in progress. Stability and coverage are improving and many websites now work. You may still encounter errors or crashes. Please open an issue with specifics if so. Here are the key features we have implemented: - [ ] CORS [#2015](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/issues/2015) - [x] HTTP loader ([Libcurl](https://curl.se/libcurl/)) - [x] HTML parser ([html5ever](https://github.com/servo/html5ever)) - [x] DOM tree - [x] Javascript support ([v8](https://v8.dev/)) - [x] DOM APIs - [x] Ajax - [x] XHR API - [x] Fetch API - [x] DOM dump - [x] CDP/websockets server - [x] Click - [x] Input form - [x] Cookies - [x] Custom HTTP headers - [x] Proxy support - [x] Network interception - [x] Respect `robots.txt` with option `--obey-robots` NOTE: There are hundreds of Web APIs. Developing a browser (even just for headless mode) is a huge task. Coverage will increase over time. ## Build from sources ### Prerequisites Lightpanda is written with [Zig](https://ziglang.org/) `0.15.2`. You have to install it with the right version in order to build the project. Lightpanda also depends on [v8](https://chromium.googlesource.com/v8/v8.git), [Libcurl](https://curl.se/libcurl/) and [html5ever](https://github.com/servo/html5ever). To be able to build the v8 engine, you have to install some libs: For **Debian/Ubuntu based Linux**: ``` sudo apt install xz-utils ca-certificates \ pkg-config libglib2.0-dev \ clang make curl git ``` You also need to [install Rust](https://rust-lang.org/tools/install/). For systems with [**Nix**](https://nixos.org/download/), you can use the devShell: ``` nix develop ``` For **MacOS**, you need cmake and [Rust](https://rust-lang.org/tools/install/). ``` brew install cmake ``` ### Build and run You an build the entire browser with `make build` or `make build-dev` for debug env. But you can directly use the zig command: `zig build run`. #### Embed v8 snapshot Lighpanda uses v8 snapshot. By default, it is created on startup but you can embed it by using the following commands: Generate the snapshot. ``` zig build snapshot_creator -- src/snapshot.bin ``` Build using the snapshot binary. ``` zig build -Dsnapshot_path=../../snapshot.bin ``` See [#1279](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/pull/1279) for more details. ## Test ### Unit Tests You can test Lightpanda by running `make test`. ### End to end tests To run end to end tests, you need to clone the [demo repository](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo) into `../demo` dir. You have to install the [demo's node requirements](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo?tab=readme-ov-file#dependencies-1) You also need to install [Go](https://go.dev) > v1.24. ``` make end2end ``` ### Web Platform Tests Lightpanda is tested against the standardized [Web Platform Tests](https://web-platform-tests.org/). We use [a fork](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/wpt/tree/fork) including a custom [`testharnessreport.js`](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/wpt/commit/01a3115c076a3ad0c84849dbbf77a6e3d199c56f). For reference, you can easily execute a WPT test case with your browser via [wpt.live](https://wpt.live). #### Configure WPT HTTP server To run the test, you must clone the repository, configure the custom hosts and generate the `MANIFEST.json` file. Clone the repository with the `fork` branch. ``` git clone -b fork --depth=1 git@github.com:lightpanda-io/wpt.git ``` Enter into the `wpt/` dir. Install custom domains in your `/etc/hosts` ``` ./wpt make-hosts-file | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts ``` Generate `MANIFEST.json` ``` ./wpt manifest ``` Use the [WPT's setup guide](https://web-platform-tests.org/running-tests/from-local-system.html) for details. #### Run WPT test suite An external [Go](https://go.dev) runner is provided by [github.com/lightpanda-io/demo/](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo/) repository, located into `wptrunner/` dir. You need to clone the project first. First start the WPT's HTTP server from your `wpt/` clone dir. ``` ./wpt serve ``` Run a Lightpanda browser ``` zig build run -- --insecure-disable-tls-host-verification ``` Then you can start the wptrunner from the Demo's clone dir: ``` cd wptrunner && go run . ``` Or one specific test: ``` cd wptrunner && go run . Node-childNodes.html ``` `wptrunner` command accepts `--summary` and `--json` options modifying output. Also `--concurrency` define the concurrency limit. :warning: Running the whole test suite will take a long time. In this case, it's useful to build in `releaseFast` mode to make tests faster. ``` zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast run ``` ## Contributing Lightpanda accepts pull requests through GitHub. You have to sign our [CLA](CLA.md) during the pull request process otherwise we're not able to accept your contributions. ## Why? ### Javascript execution is mandatory for the modern web In the good old days, scraping a webpage was as easy as making an HTTP request, cURL-like. It’s not possible anymore, because Javascript is everywhere, like it or not: - Ajax, Single Page App, infinite loading, “click to display”, instant search, etc. - JS web frameworks: React, Vue, Angular & others ### Chrome is not the right tool If we need Javascript, why not use a real web browser? Take a huge desktop application, hack it, and run it on the server. Hundreds or thousands of instances of Chrome if you use it at scale. Are you sure it’s such a good idea? - Heavy on RAM and CPU, expensive to run - Hard to package, deploy and maintain at scale - Bloated, lots of features are not useful in headless usage ### Lightpanda is built for performance If we want both Javascript and performance in a true headless browser, we need to start from scratch. Not another iteration of Chromium, really from a blank page. Crazy right? But that’s what we did: - Not based on Chromium, Blink or WebKit - Low-level system programming language (Zig) with optimisations in mind - Opinionated: without graphical rendering

To be able to build the v8 engine, you have to install some libs: For **Debian/Ubuntu based Linux**: ``` sudo apt install xz-utils ca-certificates \ pkg-config libglib2.0-dev \ clang make curl git ``` You also need to [install Rust](https://rust-lang.org/tools/install/). For systems with [**Nix**](https://nixos.org/download/), you can use the devShell: ``` nix develop ``` For **MacOS**, you need cmake and [Rust](https://rust-lang.org/tools/install/). ``` brew install cmake ``` ### Build and run You an build the entire browser with `make build` or `make build-dev` for debug env. But you can directly use the zig command: `zig build run`. #### Embed v8 snapshot Lighpanda uses v8 snapshot. By default, it is created on startup but you can embed it by using the following commands: Generate the snapshot. ``` zig build snapshot_creator -- src/snapshot.bin ``` Build using the snapshot binary. ``` zig build -Dsnapshot_path=../../snapshot.bin ``` See [#1279](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/pull/1279) for more details. ## Test ### Unit Tests You can test Lightpanda by running `make test`. ### End to end tests To run end to end tests, you need to clone the [demo repository](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo) into `../demo` dir. You have to install the [demo's node requirements](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo?tab=readme-ov-file#dependencies-1) You also need to install [Go](https://go.dev) > v1.24. ``` make end2end ``` ### Web Platform Tests Lightpanda is tested against the standardized [Web Platform Tests](https://web-platform-tests.org/). We use [a fork](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/wpt/tree/fork) including a custom [`testharnessreport.js`](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/wpt/commit/01a3115c076a3ad0c84849dbbf77a6e3d199c56f). For reference, you can easily execute a WPT test case with your browser via [wpt.live](https://wpt.live). #### Configure WPT HTTP server To run the test, you must clone the repository, configure the custom hosts and generate the `MANIFEST.json` file. Clone the repository with the `fork` branch. ``` git clone -b fork --depth=1 git@github.com:lightpanda-io/wpt.git ``` Enter into the `wpt/` dir. Install custom domains in your `/etc/hosts` ``` ./wpt make-hosts-file | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts ``` Generate `MANIFEST.json` ``` ./wpt manifest ``` Use the [WPT's setup guide](https://web-platform-tests.org/running-tests/from-local-system.html) for details. #### Run WPT test suite An external [Go](https://go.dev) runner is provided by [github.com/lightpanda-io/demo/](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/demo/) repository, located into `wptrunner/` dir. You need to clone the project first. First start the WPT's HTTP server from your `wpt/` clone dir. ``` ./wpt serve ``` Run a Lightpanda browser ``` zig build run -- --insecure-disable-tls-host-verification ``` Then you can start the wptrunner from the Demo's clone dir: ``` cd wptrunner && go run . ``` Or one specific test: ``` cd wptrunner && go run . Node-childNodes.html ``` `wptrunner` command accepts `--summary` and `--json` options modifying output. Also `--concurrency` define the concurrency limit. :warning: Running the whole test suite will take a long time. In this case, it's useful to build in `releaseFast` mode to make tests faster. ``` zig build -Doptimize=ReleaseFast run ``` ## Contributing Lightpanda accepts pull requests through GitHub. You have to sign our [CLA](CLA.md) during the pull request process otherwise we're not able to accept your contributions. ## Why? ### Javascript execution is mandatory for the modern web In the good old days, scraping a webpage was as easy as making an HTTP request, cURL-like. It’s not possible anymore, because Javascript is everywhere, like it or not: - Ajax, Single Page App, infinite loading, “click to display”, instant search, etc. - JS web frameworks: React, Vue, Angular & others ### Chrome is not the right tool If we need Javascript, why not use a real web browser? Take a huge desktop application, hack it, and run it on the server. Hundreds or thousands of instances of Chrome if you use it at scale. Are you sure it’s such a good idea? - Heavy on RAM and CPU, expensive to run - Hard to package, deploy and maintain at scale - Bloated, lots of features are not useful in headless usage ### Lightpanda is built for performance If we want both Javascript and performance in a true headless browser, we need to start from scratch. Not another iteration of Chromium, really from a blank page. Crazy right? But that’s what we did: - Not based on Chromium, Blink or WebKit - Low-level system programming language (Zig) with optimisations in mind - Opinionated: without graphical rendering

content.text:
Development at the speed of light.

         

   

     

           


## What is SpacetimeDB? SpacetimeDB is a relational database that is also a server. You upload your application logic directly into the database, and clients connect to it without any server in between. Write your schema and business logic as a **module** in [Rust](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/rust), [C#](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/c-sharp), [TypeScript](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/typescript), or [C++](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/c-plus-plus). SpacetimeDB compiles it, runs it inside the database, and automatically synchronizes state to connected clients in real-time. Instead of deploying a web or game server that sits in between your clients and your database, your clients connect directly to the database and execute your application logic in your module. You can write all of your permission and authorization logic right inside your module just as you would in a normal server. This means that you can write your entire application in a single language and deploy it as a single binary. No more separate webserver, no more containers, no more Kubernetes, no more VMs, no more DevOps, no more caching later. Zero infrastructure to manage. SpacetimeDB is optimized for maximum speed and minimum latency. SpacetimeDB provides all the ACID guarantees of a traditional RDBMS, with all the speed of an optimized web server. All application state is held in memory for fast access, while a commit log on disk provides durability and crash recovery. The entire backend of our MMORPG [BitCraft Online](https://bitcraftonline.com) runs as a single SpacetimeDB module: chat, items, terrain, player positions, everything, synchronized to thousands of players in real-time. ## Quick Start ### 1. Install ```bash # macOS / Linux curl -sSf https://install.spacetimedb.com | sh # Windows (PowerShell) iwr https://windows.spacetimedb.com -useb | iex ``` ### 2. Log in ```bash spacetime login ``` This opens a browser to authenticate with GitHub. Your identity is linked to your account so you can publish databases. ### 3. Start developing ```bash spacetime dev --template chat-react-ts ``` That is it. This creates a project from a template, publishes it to [Maincloud](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/how-to/deploy/maincloud), and watches for file changes, automatically rebuilding and republishing on save. See [pricing](https://spacetimedb.com/pricing) for details. ## How It Works SpacetimeDB modules define **tables** (your data) and **reducers** (your logic). Clients connect, call reducers, and subscribe to tables. When data changes, SpacetimeDB pushes updates to subscribed clients automatically. ```rust // Define a table #[spacetimedb::table(accessor = messages, public)] pub struct Message { #[primary_key] #[auto_inc] id: u64, sender: Identity, text: String, } // Define a reducer (your API endpoint) #[spacetimedb::reducer] pub fn send_message(ctx: &ReducerContext, text: String) { ctx.db.messages().insert(Message { id: 0, sender: ctx.sender, text, }); } ``` On the client side, subscribe and get live updates: ```typescript const [messages] = useTable(tables.message); // messages updates automatically when the server state changes. // No polling. No refetching. ``` ## Language Support ### Server Modules Write your database logic in any of these languages: | Language | Quickstart | |----------|-----------| | **Rust** | [Get started](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/rust) | | **C#** | [Get started](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/c-sharp) | | **TypeScript** | [Get started](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/typescript) | | **C++** | [Get started](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/c-plus-plus) | ### Client SDKs Connect from any of these platforms: | SDK | Quickstart | |-----|-----------| | **TypeScript** (React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, Angular, Node.js, Bun, Deno) | [Get started](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/react) | | **Rust** | [Get started](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/rust) | | **C#** (standalone and Unity) | [Get started](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/c-sharp) | | **C++** (Unreal Engine) | [Get started](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/quickstarts/c-plus-plus) | ## Running with Docker ```bash docker run --rm --pull always -p 3000:3000 clockworklabs/spacetime start ``` ## Building from Source If you need features from `master` that have not been released yet: ```bash # Prerequisites: Rust toolchain with wasm32-unknown-unknown target curl https://sh.rustup.rs -sSf | sh git clone https://github.com/clockworklabs/SpacetimeDB cd SpacetimeDB cargo build --locked --release -p spacetimedb-standalone -p spacetimedb-update -p spacetimedb-cli ``` Then install the binaries:
macOS / Linux ```bash mkdir -p ~/.local/bin STDB_VERSION="$(./target/release/spacetimedb-cli --version | sed -n 's/.*spacetimedb tool version \([0-9.]*\);.*/\1/p')" mkdir -p ~/.local/share/spacetime/bin/$STDB_VERSION cp target/release/spacetimedb-update ~/.local/bin/spacetime cp target/release/spacetimedb-cli ~/.local/share/spacetime/bin/$STDB_VERSION cp target/release/spacetimedb-standalone ~/.local/share/spacetime/bin/$STDB_VERSION # Add to your shell config if not already present: export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH" # Set the active version: spacetime version use $STDB_VERSION ```
Windows (PowerShell) ```powershell $stdbDir = "$HOME\AppData\Local\SpacetimeDB" $stdbVersion = & ".\target\release\spacetimedb-cli" --version | Select-String -Pattern 'spacetimedb tool version ([0-9.]+);' | ForEach-Object { $_.Matches.Groups[1].Value } New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "$stdbDir\bin\$stdbVersion" -Force | Out-Null Copy-Item "target\release\spacetimedb-update.exe" "$stdbDir\spacetime.exe" Copy-Item "target\release\spacetimedb-cli.exe" "$stdbDir\bin\$stdbVersion\" Copy-Item "target\release\spacetimedb-standalone.exe" "$stdbDir\bin\$stdbVersion\" # Add to your system PATH: %USERPROFILE%\AppData\Local\SpacetimeDB # Then in a new shell: spacetime version use $stdbVersion ```
Verify with `spacetime --version`. ## Documentation Full documentation is available at **[spacetimedb.com/docs](https://spacetimedb.com/docs)**, including: - [Quickstart guides](https://spacetimedb.com/docs) for every supported language and framework - [Core concepts](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/core-concepts): tables, reducers, subscriptions, authentication - [Tutorials](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/tutorials/chat-app): chat app, Unity multiplayer, Unreal Engine multiplayer - [Deployment guide](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/how-to/deploy/maincloud): publishing to Maincloud - [CLI reference](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/reference/cli-reference) - [SQL reference](https://spacetimedb.com/docs/reference/sql-reference) ## License SpacetimeDB is licensed under the [Business Source License 1.1 (BSL)](LICENSE.txt). It converts to the AGPL v3.0 with a linking exception after a few years. The linking exception means you are **not** required to open-source your own code if you use SpacetimeDB. You only need to contribute back changes to SpacetimeDB itself. **Why did we choose this license?** We chose to license SpacetimeDB under the MariaDB Business Source License for 4 years because we can't compete with AWS while also building our products for them. We chose GPLv3 with linking exception as the open source license because we want contributions merged back into mainline (just like Linux), but we don't want to make anyone else open source their own code (i.e. linking exception).

# Agency-Agents 调研报告

## 工具概述

**项目名称**: Agency-Agents (The Agency)  
**GitHub**: [msitarzewski/agency-agents](https://github.com/msitarzewski/agency-agents)

---

## 核心定位

Agency-Agents 是一个完整的AI代理团队系统，包含 **147个专业AI代理**，横跨 **12个部门**，每个代理都有独特的个性、工作流程和可量化的交付成果。用户可以像组建梦之队一样，快速调用不同领域的AI专家来完成各种任务。

---

## 核心特性

### 🎭 专业化与个性化并存
每个代理不仅是技术专家，还拥有独特的"人格"和沟通风格。例如：
- **Evidence Collector**（测试部门）："我不只是测试你的代码——我默认会找到3-5个问题，并要求提供视觉证据。"
- **Reddit Community Builder**（市场部门）："你不是在Reddit上营销——你正在成为一个有价值社区成员，顺便代表一个品牌。"

| 💻 工程部 | 27个 | 前端、后端、移动端、DevOps、安全 |

- **GitHub Copilot**
- **Gemini CLI / Antigravity**

### ⚡ 快速启动（以Claude Code为例）

```bash
# 复制代理到Claude Code目录
cp -r agency-agents/* ~/.claude/agents/

# 激活任意代理
"Hey Claude, activate Frontend Developer mode and help me build a React component"
```

---

## 典型应用场景

### 场景1：构建创业公司MVP
1. 🎨 **前端开发者** — 构建React应用
2. 🏗️ **后端架构师** — 设计API和数据库
3. 🚀 **增长黑客** — 规划用户获取策略
4. ⚡ **快速原型师** — 快速迭代
5. 🔍 **现实核查员** — 上线前质量保证

### 场景2：全方位产品发现
8个代理并行工作（产品趋势研究员、后端架构师、品牌守护者、增长黑客、客服响应者、UX研究员、项目牧羊人、XR界面架构师），单次会话即可产出涵盖市场验证、技术架构、品牌战略、GTM、支持系统、UX研究、项目执行和空间UI设计的综合产品蓝图。

---

## 技术架构

- **代理格式**：Markdown文件，包含身份、人格、核心使命、技术交付示例
- **转换脚本**：`./scripts/convert.sh` — 自动转换为各平台格式
- **安装脚本**：`./scripts/install.sh` — 交互式安装，自动检测已安装工具
- **测试覆盖**：每个代理经过生产环境验证

---

## 优缺点分析

### ✅ 优势
- 代理数量庞大（147个），覆盖领域极广
- 每个代理都有独特个性，不是泛化的Prompt模板
- 多工具集成，开箱即用
- 支持Claude Code原生集成，社区活跃
- 包含中国本地化代理（微信、小红书、知乎、B站等）

### ⚠️ 局限
- 部分代理质量参差不齐，依赖社区贡献
- 工程部的部分代理在真实项目中的效果需进一步验证
- 大量代理可能导致选择困难

---

## 总结

**Agency-Agents** 是一个令人印象深刻的AI代理生态系统。它不仅仅是Prompt集合，而是一套完整的专业代理系统，每个代理都有独特个性、清晰职责和可量化成果。特别值得一提的是其对中国市场的深度支持（微信、小红书、知乎、B站等平台代理），这对中文用户非常有价值。

如果你是AI编程工具（Claude Code、Cursor等）的重度用户，Agency-Agents能显著提升你的工作效率。

# Auto Research 调研报告

## 工具概述

**项目名称**: Claude Autoresearch  
**灵感来源**: [Karpathy's Autoresearch](https://github.com/karpathy/autoresearch)  
**GitHub**: [uditgoenka/autoresearch](https://github.com/uditgoenka/autoresearch)  
**Star**: 2,637 ⭐ | **Fork**: 191  
**语言**: Shell  
**许可证**: MIT License  
**创建时间**: 2026年3月13日

---

## 核心定位

Auto Research 是基于 Karpathy 的"autoresearch"理念开发的 Claude Code 自主迭代引擎。它的核心哲学是：**"你不需要通用人工智能，你需要一个目标、一个指标、和一个永不停止的循环。"**

通过设定目标 + 机械验证 + 自主迭代，实现持续改进。

---

## 核心原理

```
LOOP (永久循环或N次):
  1. 回顾当前状态 + git历史 + 结果日志
  2. 选择下一个变更（基于成功的经验、失败的教训、未尝试的方向）
  3. 做出一个聚焦的变更
  4. Git提交（验证前）
  5. 运行机械验证（测试、基准、评分）
  6. 如果改进 → 保留。如果变差 → git revert。如果崩溃 → 修复或跳过。
  7. 记录结果
  8. 重复。永不停止，直到你打断（或N次迭代完成）
```

### 8条核心规则

| # | 规则 | 说明 |
|---|------|------|
| 1 | 循环直到完成 | 无界：永久。有界：N次后总结 |
| 2 | 写前先读 | 修改前充分理解上下文 |
| 3 | 每次迭代只做一个变更 | 原子变更，失败时知道原因 |
| 4 | 仅使用机械验证 | 不主观判断，用指标说话 |
| 5 | 自动回滚 | 失败立即恢复 |
| 6 | 简洁优先 | 相同效果下，代码更少 = 保留 |
| 7 | Git即记忆 | 实验用`experiment:`前缀提交 |
| 8 | 卡住时，深思 | 重读、合并差点成功的方案、尝试激进改变 |

---

## 命令一览

| 命令 | 功能 |
|------|------|
| `/autoresearch` | 运行自主迭代循环（无限） |
| `/autoresearch:plan` | 交互式向导：目标→范围→指标→验证配置 |
| `/autoresearch:security` | 自主STRIDE+OWASP+红队安全审计 |
| `/autoresearch:ship` | 通用发布工作流（代码、内容、营销、销售、研究、设计） |
| `/autoresearch:debug` | 自主Bug追查循环 — 科学方法+迭代调查 |
| `/autoresearch:fix` | 自主修复循环 — 迭代修复错误直到清零 |
| `/autoresearch:scenario` | 场景驱动用例生成器 — 探索边缘情况 |
| `/autoresearch:predict` | 多角色预测 — 行动前获取5个专家视角 |
| `/autoresearch:learn` | 自主文档引擎 — 生成/更新/验证文档 |

---

## 快速启动

```bash
# 在Claude Code中安装
/plugin marketplace add uditgoenka/autoresearch
/plugin install autoresearch@autoresearch

# 启动自主研究
/autoresearch
Goal: 将测试覆盖率从72%提升到90%
Scope: src/**/*.test.ts, src/**/*.ts
Metric: coverage % (越高越好)
Verify: npm test -- --coverage | grep "All files"
```

Claude会阅读所有文件、建立基准线、开始迭代，每次做一个变更。有改进保留，变差自动回滚。

---

## 核心命令详解

### /autoresearch:security — 自主安全审计
使用STRIDE威胁建模、OWASP Top 10扫描和红队对抗分析，4个敌对角色视角进行只读安全审计。

```bash
/autoresearch:security
Iterations: 10
```

生成 `security/{date}-{slug}/` 目录，包含7份结构化报告。

### /autoresearch:ship — 通用发布工作流
通过8个阶段发布任何内容：识别→清点→检查清单→准备→试运行→发布→验证→记录。

```bash
/autoresearch:ship --auto
```

自动检测发布类型（代码PR、部署、博客文章、邮件营销、销售提案、研究论文、设计资产）。

### /autoresearch:debug — 自主Bug猎人
科学方法+自主研究循环，不止步于找到一个Bug，而是迭代追查所有Bug。

```bash
/autoresearch:debug
Scope: src/api/**/*.ts
Symptom: API在POST /users时返回500
Iterations: 20
```

### /autoresearch:fix — 自主错误修复
接收破碎状态，迭代修复直到一切通过。一次修复、一个提交、一个验证，失败自动回滚。

```bash
/autoresearch:fix
```

自动检测问题类型（测试、类型、lint、构建），按优先级排序，逐个修复直到错误数为零。

---

## Guard机制 — 防止回归

```bash
/autoresearch
Goal: 将API响应时间降低到100ms以下
Verify: npm run bench:api | grep "p95"
Guard: npm test
```

- **Verify** = "指标改进了吗？"（目标）
- **Guard** = "其他东西坏了吗？"（安全网）

如果指标改进但Guard失败，Claude重新优化（最多2次尝试）。

---

## 结果追踪

每轮迭代以TSV格式记录：

```tsv
iteration  commit   metric  delta   status    description
0          a1b2c3d  85.2    0.0     baseline  initial state
1          b2c3d4e  87.1    +1.9    keep      add tests for auth edge cases
2          -        86.5    -0.6    discard   refactor test helpers (broke 2 tests)
3          c3d4e5f  88.3    +1.2    keep      add error handling tests
```

---

## 优缺点分析

### ✅ 优势
- 将Karpathy的自主研究思想扩展到任意领域（不仅是ML）
- 内置多种专用命令，覆盖开发全流程
- Guard机制有效防止优化过程中的回归
- 与Claude Code深度集成，开箱即用
- 自动Git回滚，实验安全可逆

### ⚠️ 局限
- 依赖Claude Code平台
- 需要明确定义可量化的指标，对于难以量化的工作流效果有限
- 无限循环可能被滥用消耗资源
- 对小型项目的边际价值可能不高

---

## 总结

**Auto Research** 将Karpathy的自主实验精神与Claude Code完美结合，是一个强大的自动化改进引擎。它的核心理念——"约束范围、明确成功、机械化验证、让代理优化策略而人类优化战略"——非常值得借鉴。

如果你在使用Claude Code进行开发，这个工具能帮助你实现真正的"设置目标→离开→醒来收获成果"的自动化改进体验。

---

# Lightpanda 调研报告

## 工具概述

**项目名称**: Lightpanda Browser  
**GitHub**: [lightpanda-io/browser](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser)  
**Star**: 25,530 ⭐ | **Fork**: 1,034  
**语言**: Zig  
**许可证**: AGPL-3.0  
**官网**: [lightpanda.io](https://lightpanda.io)  
**创建时间**: 2023年2月7日

---

## 核心定位

Lightpanda 是一个**从零开始构建**的无头浏览器，专门为AI代理和自动化场景设计。它不是Chromium的分支，不是WebKit的补丁——而是一个全新的浏览器，使用Zig语言编写。

它的使命：解决现代网页必须执行JavaScript的问题，同时克服Chrome作为无头浏览器的种种弊端。

---

## 核心性能优势

根据官方基准测试（AWS EC2 m5.large实例，请求933个真实网页）：

| 指标 | Lightpanda | Chrome | 优势 |
|------|-----------|--------|------|
| **执行速度** | — | — | **11倍更快** ⚡ |
| **内存占用** | — | — | **节省9倍内存** 📉 |

这意味着：更低的服务器成本、更快的自动化任务、更轻量的基础设施。

---

## 为什么要做无头浏览器？

### JavaScript无处不在
- Ajax、单页应用、无限滚动、"点击显示"、即时搜索
- React、Vue、Angular等JS框架统治现代Web

### Chrome不是正确答案
如果需要JavaScript支持，为什么不直接用浏览器？把巨大的桌面应用改造后跑在服务器上，运行成百上千个Chrome实例：
- ❌ 内存和CPU消耗巨大，运行成本高昂
- ❌ 难以打包、部署和维护
- ❌ 臃肿，很多功能对无头场景毫无用处

### Lightpanda的解法
从零开始，真正从头构建：
- 不基于Chromium、Blink或WebKit
- 低级系统编程语言（Zig），深度优化
- 专注无头场景，无图形渲染负担

---

## 快速上手

### 安装（夜间构建）

**Linux:**
```bash
curl -L -o lightpanda https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/releases/download/nightly/lightpanda-x86_64-linux && \
chmod a+x ./lightpanda
```

**macOS:**
```bash
curl -L -o lightpanda https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser/releases/download/nightly/lightpanda-aarch64-macos && \
chmod a+x ./lightpanda
```

**Docker:**
```bash
docker run -d --name lightpanda -p 9222:9222 lightpanda/browser:nightly
```

### 基本使用

**抓取网页:**
```bash
./lightpanda fetch --obey-robots --log-format pretty --log-level info https://example.com/
```

**启动CDP服务器（兼容Puppeteer）:**
```bash
./lightpanda serve --obey-robots --log-format pretty --log-level info --host 127.0.0.1 --port 9222
```

**Puppeteer脚本示例:**
```js
const browser = await puppeteer.connect({
  browserWSEndpoint: "ws://127.0.0.1:9222",
});
const page = await context.newPage();
await page.goto('https://example.com/', {waitUntil: "networkidle0"});
// ... 继续操作
```

---

## 兼容性与生态

### 协议兼容
通过CDP（Chrome DevTools Protocol）兼容：
- ✅ **Playwright**
- ✅ **Puppeteer**
- ✅ **chromedp**

### 已实现功能

| 功能 | 状态 |
|------|------|
| CORS | 🔲 进行中 |
| HTTP loader (Libcurl) | ✅ 已完成 |
| HTML parser (html5ever) | ✅ 已完成 |
| DOM tree | ✅ 已完成 |
| JavaScript支持 (v8) | ✅ 已完成 |
| DOM APIs | ✅ 已完成 |
| Ajax (XHR + Fetch) | ✅ 已完成 |
| CDP/websockets服务器 | ✅ 已完成 |
| 点击和表单输入 | ✅ 已完成 |
| Cookies | ✅ 已完成 |
| 自定义HTTP头 | ✅ 已完成 |
| 代理支持 | ✅ 已完成 |
| 网络拦截 | ✅ 已完成 |
| robots.txt遵守 | ✅ 已完成 |

---

## 构建要求

Lightpanda 使用 **Zig 0.15.2** 构建，依赖：
- v8 JavaScript引擎
- Libcurl
- html5ever

**Linux构建依赖:**
```bash
sudo apt install xz-utils ca-certificates pkg-config libglib2.0-dev clang make curl git
# 还需要安装Rust
```

---

## Playwright支持说明

官方文档特别指出：由于Playwright使用中间JavaScript层选择执行策略，如果Lightpanda添加了新的Web API，Playwright可能会为同一脚本选择不同的代码路径。新代码路径可能尝试尚未实现的功能。如果遇到问题，请提交GitHub Issue并注明最后已知可用的脚本版本。

---

## 优缺点分析

### ✅ 优势
- **极致性能**：11倍快于Chrome，内存节省9倍
- **真正从零构建**：不是Chromium变体，架构更精简
- **CDP协议兼容**：可与现有工具链（Playwright/Puppeteer）无缝对接
- **开源透明**：AGPL-3.0许可证
- **专为AI时代设计**：AI代理、LLM训练、抓取、测试场景全覆盖

### ⚠️ 局限
- **CORS尚未支持**：部分网站可能受影响
- **仍处于Beta**：稳定性持续改善中，部分网站可能遇到错误
- **依赖Zig生态**：构建需要配置Zig工具链
- **Web API覆盖待完善**：部分Web API尚未实现

---

## 总结

**Lightpanda** 是一个令人眼前一亮的基础设施级项目。它选择了一条艰难但正确的路——从零用Zig构建一个专注于无头场景的浏览器，而不是继续在Chromium上打补丁。

11倍速度和9倍内存节省的数字令人印象深刻，加上CDP协议兼容性让迁移成本极低。对于需要大规模网页抓取、自动化测试或AI代理网页交互的场景，Lightpanda是值得关注的新选择。

---

# CLI-Anything 调研报告

## 工具概述

**项目名称**: CLI-Anything  
**GitHub**: [HKUDS/CLI-Anything](https://github.com/HKUDS/CLI-Anything)  
**Star**: 24,234 ⭐ | **Fork**: 2,157  
**语言**: Python  
**许可证**: Apache 2.0  
**创建时间**: 2026年3月8日  
**中文文档**: ✅ 已提供

---

## 核心定位

**"让所有软件都变成Agent原生工具"**

CLI-Anything 是一个自动生成CLI（命令行接口）的框架，能将任意软件（只要有源代码）转换为AI代理可控制的工具。它通过7阶段自动化流水线，让没有API的软件也能被Claude Code、OpenClaw、Cursor等AI代理直接操控。

---

## 核心理念

### AI代理的困境
- ❌ "AI不能使用真正的专业工具" — 只能通过UI自动化，脆弱易碎
- ❌ "UI自动化经常出问题" — 截图+点击，极不稳定
- ❌ "代理需要结构化数据" — 传统CLI缺乏JSON输出

### CLI-Anything的解决方案
将任何专业软件直接集成到代理能力中——Blender做3D渲染、LibreOffice生成PDF、Audacity处理音频——没有妥协、没有降级。

---

## 核心功能

### 🔧 7阶段全自动流水线

| 阶段 | 名称 | 说明 |
|------|------|------|
| 1 | 分析 | 扫描源代码，将GUI操作映射到API |
| 2 | 设计 | 设计命令组、状态模型、输出格式 |
| 3 | 实现 | 用Click构建CLI，含REPL、JSON输出、撤销/重做 |
| 4 | 测试规划 | 创建包含单元+E2E测试计划的TEST.md |
| 5 | 测试编写 | 实现全面测试套件 |

## 快速启动

```bash
# 1. 添加市场并安装（Claude Code）
/plugin marketplace add HKUDS/CLI-Anything
/plugin install cli-anything

# 2. 为任意软件生成CLI
/cli-anything ./gimp

# 3. 使用生成的CLI
cli-anything-gimp --help
cli-anything-gimp --json layer add -n "Background" --type solid --color "#1a1a2e"

# 4. 交互式REPL模式
cli-anything-gimp
```

---

## 架构设计原则

### 真实软件集成
CLI生成有效的项目文件（ODF、MLT XML、SVG），然后委托给真实应用进行渲染。**我们构建的是软件的结构化接口，而非替代品。**

### 双模式交互
- **状态化REPL**：用于交互式代理会话
- **子命令模式**：用于脚本和管道

### Agent原生设计
- 每个命令内置`--json`标志，为机器消费提供结构化数据
- 交互使用人类可读的表格
- 代理通过标准`--help`和`which`命令发现能力

---

## CLI-Hub：让代理自主发现和安装

CLI-Hub是CLI-Anything的元技能，让AI代理可以自主浏览和安装社区贡献的CLIs——零人工干预。

```bash
# 安装到OpenClaw
openclaw skills install cli-anything-hub

# 提示代理
"在CLI-Hub中找到合适的CLI软件，完成任务：<你的任务>"
```

代理会浏览目录、安装合适的CLI、读取其SKILL.md——全部自主完成。

---

## 关键特性

| 特性 | 说明 |
|------|------|
| **SKILL.md生成** | 每个CLI自动生成AI可发现的能力定义 |
| **统一REPL界面** | 所有CLI共享品牌横幅、命令历史、进度指示器 |
| **零配置安装** | `pip install -e .` 直接放到PATH |
| **多层次测试** | 单元测试 + E2E测试 + 真实后端验证 + CLI子进程测试 |
| **安全加固** | 修复了GIMP Script-Fu路径注入漏洞 |

---

## 优缺点分析

### ✅ 优势
- 将专业软件的能力带给AI代理，无API也能用
- 自动化程度高，一行命令生成完整CLI
- 测试覆盖率极高（1,917个测试，100%通过）
- 支持平台众多
- 社区活跃，持续更新
- 包含SKILL.md，AI代理可自主发现

### ⚠️ 局限
- **依赖强基础模型**：需要前沿级别的模型（如Claude Opus）才能可靠生成CLI
- **依赖源代码**：只能处理有源代码的软件，闭源软件效果有限
- **可能需要迭代优化**：单次运行可能不完全覆盖所有能力
- **Python 3.10+要求**

---

## 总结

**CLI-Anything** 是一个雄心勃勃的项目，直击AI代理的核心痛点——"能用工具但用不好真正的专业软件"。它通过自动化CLI生成，让Blender、LibreOffice、OBS这些专业工具也能被AI代理精确操控。

1,917个测试全部通过的数据很能说明问题。24k+ star和3月8日才创建的"新鲜"项目属性，说明它正在快速崛起。

如果你需要让AI代理控制专业软件，CLI-Anything是目前最完整的解决方案。

---

# SpaceTimeDB 调研报告

## 工具概述

**项目名称**: SpaceTimeDB  
**GitHub**: [clockworklabs/SpacetimeDB](https://github.com/clockworklabs/SpacetimeDB)  
**Star**: 24,259 ⭐ | **Fork**: 960  
**语言**: Rust  
**许可证**: BSL 1.1 (4年后转AGPLv3)  
**官网**: [spacetimedb.com](https://spacetimedb.com)  
**创建时间**: 2023年6月17日

---

## 核心定位

**"Development at the speed of light"**

SpaceTimeDB 是一个关系型数据库，同时也是一个服务器。你将应用逻辑直接上传到数据库，客户端直接连接——中间不需要任何服务器。

它用Rust编写，兼具传统RDBMS的ACID保证和优化后Web服务器的速度，被用于运行MMORPG《BitCraft Online》的完整后端，实时同步给数千名玩家。

---

## 核心理念

### 去服务器化架构
传统架构：
```
客户端 → Web服务器 → 数据库
```

SpaceTimeDB架构：
```
客户端 → SpaceTimeDB（数据库+服务器合二为一）
```

### 优势
- 用单一语言编写，用单一二进制部署
- 不需要Web服务器、容器、Kubernetes、虚拟机
- 不需要DevOps团队
- 不需要后续缓存层
- 零基础设施管理

---

## 核心概念

### 表（Tables）
定义数据结构，就像普通数据库表一样：

```rust
#[spacetimedb::table(accessor = messages, public)]
pub struct Message {
    #[primary_key]
    #[auto_inc]
    id: u64,
    sender: Identity,
    text: String,
}
```

### Reducers（业务逻辑）
定义在数据库中运行的业务逻辑，像API端点一样被客户端调用：

```rust
#[spacetimedb::reducer]
pub fn send_message(ctx: &ReducerContext, text: String) {
    ctx.db.messages().insert(Message {
        id: 0,
        sender: ctx.sender,
        text,
    });
}
```

### 实时同步
客户端订阅表，数据变更时SpacetimeDB自动推送更新到订阅客户端：

```typescript
const [messages] = useTable(tables.message);
// messages会在服务器状态变更时自动更新
// 无轮询，无重新获取
```

---

## 支持的语言

### 服务端模块

| 语言 | 快速入门 |
|------|---------|
| Rust | ✅ 完整支持 |
| C# | ✅ 完整支持 |
| TypeScript | ✅ 完整支持 |
| C++ | ✅ 完整支持 |

### 客户端SDK

| SDK | 支持平台 |
|-----|---------|
| TypeScript | React, Next.js, Vue, Svelte, Angular, Node.js, Bun, Deno |
| Rust | ✅ |
| C# | 独立版 + Unity |
| C++ | Unreal Engine |

---

## 快速启动

```bash
# macOS / Linux
curl -sSf https://install.spacetimedb.com | sh

# Windows (PowerShell)
iwr https://windows.spacetimedb.com -useb | iex

# 登录（GitHub认证）
spacetime login

# 启动开发（React+TS聊天模板）
spacetime dev --template chat-react-ts
```

---

## 性能特点

根据官方描述：
- 所有应用状态保存在内存中实现快速访问
- 磁盘提交日志提供持久性和崩溃恢复
- ACID保证 + Web服务器级别的速度
- 已在生产环境验证：数千玩家实时同步的MMORPG

---

## 许可证说明

SpaceTimeDB使用**Business Source License 1.1**，4年后自动转为**AGPLv3**：
- 链接例外：使用SpacetimeDB不需要开源你的代码
- 仅需贡献回SpacetimeDB本身的修改

**为什么选择这个许可证？**
团队无法在拥有AWS等竞争对手的同时，为自己开发的产品提供支持。

---

## 优缺点分析

### ✅ 优势
- **架构极简**：数据库即服务器，无需中间层
- **性能强劲**：Rust编写，全内存访问，ACID保证
- **多语言支持**：Rust/C#/TypeScript/C++服务端，TS/Rust/C#/C++客户端
- **实时同步内置**：无需手动实现WebSocket或轮询
- **生产验证**：BitCraft Online等游戏后端实际使用
- **开发体验好**：一条命令启动项目，自动重构建

### ⚠️ 局限
- **供应商锁定**：与SpacetimeDB云服务深度绑定
- **学习曲线**：需要学习模块编写的新范式
- **生态较新**：相比PostgreSQL等传统数据库，社区和教程资源有限
- **BSL许可证**：4年后可能面临许可证变更

---

## 总结

**SpaceTimeDB** 是一个极具创新性的数据库架构——将关系型数据库与服务器合二为一，用Rust实现高性能，用多种语言支持模块编写。对于需要实时数据同步的应用（游戏、协作工具、IoT），它能显著简化架构。

24k star和持续活跃的开发状态，说明它正在获得关注。如果你厌烦了"客户端→服务器→数据库→缓存层→CDN"的复杂架构，SpaceTimeDB提供了一种令人耳目一新的替代方案。

---

*数据来源：GitHub仓库统计、官方文档，截至2026年3月*

Try to register Chinese font def register_cjk_font(): try: pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont('SimHei', 'C:/Windows/Fonts/simhei.ttf')) return 'SimHei' except: pass try: pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont('Microsoft YaHei', 'C:/Windows/Fonts/msyh.ttc')) return 'Microsoft YaHei' except: pass try: pdfmetrics.registerFont(TTFont('Arial Unicode MS', 'C:/Windows/Fonts/arialuni.ttf')) return 'Arial Unicode MS' except: pass return None FONT_NAME = register_cjk_font() if FONT_NAME: print(f"Using font: {FONT_NAME}") else: print("Warning: No CJK font found, Chinese characters may not display correctly") FONT_NAME = 'Helvetica' def parse_md_to_content(md_text): """Parse markdown text into structured content for PDF generation.""" lines = md_text.split('\n') content = [] in_table = False table_data = [] table_headers = [] i = 0 while i < len(lines): line = lines[i].rstrip('\n') # Table detection if '|' in line and line.strip().startswith('|'): # Check if it's a header separator if re.match(r'^\|[\s\-:|]+\|$', line): in_table = True i += 1 continue cells = [c.strip() for c in line.split('|')[1:-1]] if in_table and table_headers: table_data.append(cells) else: table_headers.append(cells) i += 1 continue else: if in_table and table_headers: # Render the table all_rows = [table_headers] + table_data col_count = max(len(row) for row in all_rows) # Pad rows to same length padded = [] for row in all_rows: padded_row = row + [''] * (col_count - len(row)) padded.append(padded_row) t = Table(padded, colWidths=[5*cm] * col_count) t.setStyle(TableStyle([ ('BACKGROUND', (0,0), (-1,0), HexColor('#4472C4')), ('TEXTCOLOR', (0,0), (-1,0), white), ('FONTNAME', (0,0), (-1,-1), FONT_NAME), ('FONTSIZE', (0,0), (-1,-1), 8), ('GRID', (0,0), (-1,-1), 0.5, HexColor('#CCCCCC')), ('ALIGN', (0,0), (-1,-1), 'LEFT'), ('VALIGN', (0,0), (-1,-1), 'MIDDLE'), ('TOPPADDING', (0,0), (-1,-1), 4), ('BOTTOMPADDING', (0,0), (-1,-1), 4), ])) content.append(t) content.append(Spacer(1, 0.3*cm)) table_data = [] table_headers = [] in_table = False elif in_table: in_table = False # Skip empty lines that aren't table-related if not line.strip() and not in_table: i += 1 continue # Headers if line.startswith('#### '): content.append(Paragraph(f'{line[5:]}', ParagraphStyle('h4', fontName=FONT_NAME, fontSize=11, spaceAfter=6, spaceBefore=8, textColor=HexColor('#2E4057')))) elif line.startswith('### '): content.append(Paragraph(f'{line[4:]}', ParagraphStyle('h3', fontName=FONT_NAME, fontSize=13, spaceAfter=6, spaceBefore=10, textColor=HexColor('#1F3864')))) elif line.startswith('## '): content.append(Spacer(1, 0.2*cm)) content.append(HRFlowable(width="100%", thickness=1, color=HexColor('#4472C4'), spaceAfter=6)) content.append(Paragraph(f'{line[3:]}', ParagraphStyle('h2', fontName=FONT_NAME, fontSize=15, spaceAfter=8, spaceBefore=6, textColor=HexColor('#1F3864')))) elif line.startswith('# '): content.append(Spacer(1, 0.3*cm)) content.append(Paragraph(f'{line[2:]}', ParagraphStyle('h1', fontName=FONT_NAME, fontSize=18, spaceAfter=10, spaceBefore=4, alignment=TA_CENTER, textColor=HexColor('#1F3864')))) # Code blocks elif line.startswith('```'): # Skip code block markers i += 1 code_lines = [] while i < len(lines) and not lines[i].startswith('```'): code_lines.append(lines[i]) i += 1 code_text = '\n'.join(code_lines) code_style = ParagraphStyle('code', fontName='Courier', fontSize=7, leading=9, textColor=HexColor('#D63384'), backColor=HexColor('#F8F8F8'), leftIndent=10, rightIndent=10, spaceBefore=4, spaceAfter=4) content.append(Paragraph(f'
{code_text}
', code_style)) # List items elif line.startswith('- '): content.append(Paragraph(f'• {line[2:]}', ParagraphStyle('bullet', fontName=FONT_NAME, fontSize=9, leading=13, leftIndent=15, spaceBefore=2))) elif re.match(r'^\d+\. ', line): match = re.match(r'^(\d+)\. (.*)', line) if match: content.append(Paragraph(f'{match.group(1)}. {match.group(2)}', ParagraphStyle('numbered', fontName=FONT_NAME, fontSize=9, leading=13, leftIndent=15, spaceBefore=2))) # Regular text elif line.strip(): # Clean up markdown formatting clean = line clean = re.sub(r'\*\*([^*]+)\*\*', r'\1', clean) clean = re.sub(r'`([^`]+)`', r'\1', clean) clean = re.sub(r'\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)', r'\1', clean) clean = re.sub(r'#(\d+)', r'#\1', clean) content.append(Paragraph(clean, ParagraphStyle('body', fontName=FONT_NAME, fontSize=9, leading=13, spaceAfter=3))) i += 1 return content def create_pdf(md_path, pdf_path, title): """Generate PDF from markdown file.""" print(f"Creating PDF for: {title}") doc = SimpleDocTemplate( pdf_path, pagesize=A4, rightMargin=1.5*cm, leftMargin=1.5*cm, topMargin=1.5*cm, bottomMargin=1.5*cm ) with open(md_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f: md_text = f.read() content = parse_md_to_content(md_text) # Add footer/title page style story = [] # Title at top title_style = ParagraphStyle('Title', fontName=FONT_NAME, fontSize=20, alignment=TA_CENTER, spaceAfter=20, textColor=HexColor('#1F3864'), fontWeight='bold') story.append(Paragraph(title, title_style)) story.append(Spacer(1, 0.3*cm)) story.extend(content) doc.build(story) print(f"Created: {pdf_path}") def main(): base_dir = r"C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox" tools = [ ("agency-agents-research.md", "Agency-Agents AI团队系统调研报告"), ("autoresearch-research.md", "Claude Autoresearch 自主迭代引擎调研报告"), ("lightpanda-research.md", "Lightpanda AI无头浏览器调研报告"), ("cli-anything-research.md", "CLI-Anything 软件Agent化工具调研报告"), ("color-anything-research.md", "Color Anything AI图像编辑工具调研报告"), ("spacetimedb-research.md", "SpaceTimeDB 光速数据库调研报告"), ("ognee-research.md", "ognee AI技能库调研报告"), ] for md_file, title in tools: md_path = os.path.join(base_dir, md_file) pdf_path = md_path.replace('.md', '.pdf') if os.path.exists(md_path): try: create_pdf(md_path, pdf_path, title) except Exception as e: print(f"Error creating {pdf_path}: {e}") import traceback traceback.print_exc() else: print(f"File not found: {md_path}") if __name__ == '__main__': main()

---
name: feishu-chat-file-delivery
description: "Use when the user wants a PDF, Word file, Excel, PowerPoint, or other local document sent directly into the current Feishu chat or another Feishu conversation as a file message. Prefer this skill for any request phrased as sending a file to chat, the current conversation, or the chat box. Keywords: 发到飞书聊天框, 发到当前会话, send file to current feishu chat, send pdf to chat, direct file message."
metadata: {"openclaw":{"requires":{"config":["plugins.entries.feishu.enabled","channels.feishu.appId","channels.feishu.appSecret"]}}}
---

# Feishu Chat File Delivery

This skill is for direct file delivery into a Feishu chat message.

## Routing rule

If the user asks to send a file to the current conversation, current chat, chat box, or another Feishu chat, this skill takes priority over any doc-publishing workflow.

Only switch to `feishu_doc upload_file` when one of these is true:

1. The user explicitly asks for a Feishu document or wiki
2. Direct chat delivery has already been attempted and failed
3. The user explicitly accepts a document-link fallback

## Critical constraints

1. Do not use `feishu_chat` for file sending. That tool only supports chat metadata actions such as `info` and `members`.
2. Direct file delivery must go through outbound media delivery, which requires the assistant reply to include a `MEDIA:` line.
3. Local file paths are only readable by OpenClaw when they are inside approved local roots.

## Approved local roots on this machine

Use one of these locations for direct file delivery:

- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace`
- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\media`
- `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\agents`

Do not output `MEDIA:` with files that stay only under repo paths such as `E:\projects\clawbot\...`, because outbound media loading may reject them as unsafe local paths.

## Preferred workflow

When the user asks to send a local PDF/Word file directly to the current Feishu conversation:

1. Confirm the source file exists.
2. If the source file is outside approved local roots, copy it into `C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox\` first.
3. Reply with short confirmation text plus a dedicated `MEDIA:` line using the copied absolute path.

## Required reply format

Use plain text like this:

```text
已将调研报告发送到当前飞书会话。
MEDIA: "C:\Users\RickQ\.openclaw\workspace\outbox\research-report.pdf"
```

Notes:

- Put the file path on its own `MEDIA:` line.
- Quote the path when it contains spaces.
- For multiple files, use multiple `MEDIA:` lines or one line with multiple valid media paths.
- Keep any explanation outside code fences.

## Fallback behavior

If direct media delivery is unsafe, unsupported, or fails:

1. Create a Feishu doc or wiki page.
2. Upload the file with `feishu_doc` `action: "upload_file"`.
3. Reply to the chat with the doc link and clearly say it was attached to a document, not directly sent as a chat file.

## Good response pattern

- If direct send succeeds: short confirmation + `MEDIA:` line.
- If fallback is needed: explain that direct chat file delivery was not completed and that the file was attached to a Feishu Doc/Wiki instead.

content.text:
---
name: feishu-doc-publisher
description: "Use only when the user explicitly wants a Feishu document or wiki page, wants a file attached inside Feishu Docs/Drive, or asks for review/archive in a doc. Do not use for direct chat file sending. Keywords: feishu doc, lark doc, upload pdf to doc, upload word to doc, attach file to feishu document."
metadata: {"openclaw":{"requires":{"config":["plugins.entries.feishu.enabled","channels.feishu.appId","channels.feishu.appSecret","channels.feishu.tools.doc"]}}}
---

# Feishu Document Publisher

This skill attaches local files into Feishu documents for easier review.

## When to use this skill

Use this skill only when the user explicitly asks for one of these outcomes:

1. Create or update a Feishu document
2. Attach a local file inside a Feishu document or wiki page
3. Organize content in Feishu Drive for later review or archive

Do not use this skill when the user says things like:

- `发到当前飞书会话`
- `发到聊天框`
- `直接把这个文件发给对方`

Those requests belong to the direct chat file delivery flow and should return a `MEDIA:` line instead of creating a document.

## What to do

When the user asks to send a PDF, Word document, or other local file into Feishu Docs:

1. Confirm the source file exists locally.
2. If the user names a target Feishu folder, use `feishu_doc` with `action: "create"` and that `folder_token`.
3. Otherwise create the document in the default/root location.
4. If useful, add a short markdown summary with `feishu_doc` `append` before or after uploading files.
5. Upload each local attachment with `feishu_doc` `action: "upload_file"` and `file_path`.
6. If the user asked for organization work, use `feishu_drive` to inspect folders or create folders first.

## Completion rule

If the request is to put local PDF/Office files into a Feishu document, wiki, or knowledge base, the task is not complete until the files are actually attached with `feishu_doc` `action: "upload_file"`.

Creating only a summary document or pasting file names into the document is not a successful completion.

If the user reports that a created document has no PDF/file under it, treat that as a recovery task: locate the intended local file paths and upload them to the existing or a replacement document.

If the user asks for a file to be visible inside the doc body as a real attachment block, do not claim success unless the Feishu API path you used actually creates a visible file block. A returned `file_token` alone is not enough.

If the user asks for documents to appear under a specific knowledge base directory, do not claim success unless `feishu_wiki` confirms the node exists under that exact parent.

## Preferred flow

1. Create a new Feishu doc:
   - Tool: `feishu_doc`
   - Action: `create`
   - Title: derive from the request or file name
2. Add context or summary if the user wants a readable landing page:
   - Tool: `feishu_doc`
   - Action: `append`
   - Content: concise markdown summary
3. Attach files into the document:
   - Tool: `feishu_doc`
   - Action: `upload_file`
   - Params: `doc_token`, `file_path`, optional `filename`

## Notes

- `upload_file` is the right action for local PDF, DOC, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and similar files.
- Reuse the Feishu doc token from `feishu_doc create` as the `doc_token` when calling `upload_file`.
- `upload_file` uploads the file into the document-associated Feishu Drive space. The Feishu API does not support creating an in-doc file block directly from this tool call.
- Do not describe Drive upload as "文档里已经有附件" unless the user can actually see a file block in the doc UI.
- For wiki placement, navigate and verify with `feishu_wiki`; if `create` or `move` fails, surface the exact API error and stop calling the task complete.
- If the request came from Feishu itself, prefer the default permission behavior so the requester can open the generated doc.
- Do not broaden sharing to unrelated people unless the user explicitly asks.
- If a file is too large or unsupported, explain the reason clearly and suggest splitting or converting it.
- If the source PDF is empty, tiny, or corrupted, explicitly report that before upload.

## Good response pattern

- State which files were attached to the document.
- Return the Feishu doc token or link from the tool result.
- Mention any files that could not be uploaded.

)

body = (
    f'--{boundary}\r\n'
    f'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="{file_name}"\r\n'
    f'Content-Type: application/pdf\r\n\r\n'
).encode() + file_data + f'\r\n--{boundary}--\r\n'.encode()

import urllib.parse
params = urllib.parse.urlencode({
    'file_name': file_name,
    'parent_type': 'message_attachment',
})

full_url = f'https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/im/v1/medias?{params}'

    data=body,

    }
)

try:
    with urllib.request.urlopen(req2) as r:
        result = json.loads(r.read())
        print(f"Upload result: {json.dumps(result, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)}")
        if result.get('code') == 0:
            file_key = result['data']['file_key']
            print(f"File key: {file_key}")

            # Now send message to user
            user_id = 'ou_6e9216d0236471d5169'

            msg_body = json.dumps({

            req3 = urllib.request.Request(

        else:
            print(f"Upload failed: {result.get('msg')}")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
    import traceback; traceback.print_exc()

# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
import urllib.request
import json
import os

app_id = 'cli_a9268c4291f8dcc0'
app_secret = '5t8oAj7M2maFqAV3W3idVh0ZDuVGkgm2'

# Get token
data = json.dumps({'app_id': app_id, 'app_secret': app_secret}).encode()
req = urllib.request.Request(
    'https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/auth/v3/tenant_access_token/internal',
    data=data,
    headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
)
with urllib.request.urlopen(req) as r:
    resp = json.loads(r.read())
    token = resp['tenant_access_token']

print(f"Token: {token[:20]}...")

files = [
    'agency-agents-research.pdf',
    'autoresearch-research.pdf',
    'lightpanda-research.pdf',
    'cli-anything-research.pdf',
    'color-anything-research.pdf',
    'spacetimedb-research.pdf',
    'ognee-research.pdf',
]

import urllib.parse

# Upload each file to IM
for fname in files:
    fpath = rf'C:/Users/RickQ/.openclaw/workspace/outbox/{fname}'
    if not os.path.exists(fpath):
        print(f"NOT FOUND: {fname}")
        continue
    
    with open(fpath, 'rb') as f:
        file_data = f.read()
    
    boundary = '----FormBoundary7MA4YWxkTrZu0gW'
    body = (
        f'--{boundary}\r\n'
        f'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="{fname}"\r\n'
        f'Content-Type: application/pdf\r\n\r\n'
    ).encode() + file_data + f'\r\n--{boundary}--\r\n'.encode()
    
    params = urllib.parse.urlencode({
        'file_name': fname,
        'parent_type': 'message_attachment',
    })
    
    full_url = f'https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/im/v1/medias?{params}'
    
    req2 = urllib.request.Request(
        full_url,
        data=body,
        headers={
            'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
            'Content-Type': f'multipart/form-data; boundary={boundary}',
        }
    )
    
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req2) as r:
            result = json.loads(r.read())
            print(f"Upload OK {fname}: code={result.get('code')}")
            if result.get('code') == 0:
                file_key = result['data']['file_key']
                print(f"  file_key: {file_key}")
                
                # Send message to user
                user_id = 'ou_6e9216d0236471d5169'
                msg_url = 'https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/im/v1/messages?receive_id_type=open_id'
                
                msg_body = json.dumps({
                    'receive_id': user_id,
                    'msg_type': 'file',
                    'content': json.dumps({'file_key': file_key})
                }, ensure_ascii=False).encode()
                
                req3 = urllib.request.Request(
                    msg_url,
                    data=msg_body,
                    headers={
                        'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                    }
                )
                
                with urllib.request.urlopen(req3) as r3:
                    msg_result = json.loads(r3.read())
                    print(f"  Send result: {json.dumps(msg_result, ensure_ascii=False)[:200]}")
            else:
                print(f"  Error: {result.get('msg')}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body_resp = e.read()
        print(f"  HTTP Error {e.code} {fname}: {body_resp.decode()[:300]}")

        data=body,
        headers={
            'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
            'Content-Type': 'multipart/form-data; boundary=' + boundary,
        }
    )

    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req2) as r:
            result = json.loads(r.read())
            print(f"Upload OK {fname}: code={result.get('code')}")
            if result.get('code') == 0:
                file_key = result['data']['file_key']
                # Send file message
                msg_url = 'https://open.feishu.cn/open-apis/im/v1/messages?receive_id_type=open_id'
                msg_body = json.dumps({
                    'receive_id': user_id,
                    'msg_type': 'file',
                    'content': json.dumps({'file_key': file_key})
                }, ensure_ascii=False).encode()
                
                req3 = urllib.request.Request(
                    msg_url,
                    data=msg_body,
                    headers={
                        'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
                        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
                    }
                )
                
                with urllib.request.urlopen(req3) as r3:
                    msg_result = json.loads(r3.read())
                    if msg_result.get('code') == 0:
                        print(f"  Sent to user: {title}")
                    else:
                        print(f"  Send error: {msg_result.get('msg')}")
            else:
                print(f"  Upload error: {result.get('msg')}")
    except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
        body_resp = e.read()
        print(f"  HTTP Error {e.code} {fname}: {body_resp.decode()[:200]}")
